title: The Meeting You Cannot Avoid
pressure: Regional has asked for a documented repair conversation between the doctor and assistant. The patient complaint made the pattern visible. Both people know something is wrong, but neither has named the trust breakdown directly.
tradeoff: You can keep the meeting controlled and safe, or let it become real enough to create discomfort.

action_contract.description: Facilitate a repair conversation that either names the trust breakdown or contains it as a communication issue.

air_logic.success: The real breakdown is acknowledged and a reset becomes possible.
air_logic.miss: The meeting is completed, but the same pattern continues under a cleaner surface.

forced_reset.trigger: Regional requires a documented repair conversation after the external complaint exposed the pattern.
forced_reset.action: You must lead the meeting and decide how real the conversation becomes.

choices.label: Open the meeting by naming the trust breakdown directly
choices.subtext: This creates immediate discomfort, but it aligns the room around the real issue.

choices.label: Frame the meeting around communication improvement
choices.subtext: This keeps the tone positive and avoids putting anyone on the defensive.

choices.label: Ask each person to share their perspective separately first
choices.subtext: This feels structured and safer because it reduces direct tension.

choices.label: Focus the meeting on future workflow instead of past tension
choices.subtext: This moves the team forward without reopening what happened.

A.escalation_text: When you name the trust breakdown, the doctor says the assistant’s inconsistency created the problem. The assistant becomes quiet and watches whether you will soften the issue.

second_choices.label: Soften the wording and return to general communication expectations
second_choices.cost: The meeting feels safer, but the trust issue becomes vague again.

second_choices.label: Hold the frame and separate impact from blame
second_choices.cost: The room stays uncomfortable, and the doctor may feel challenged.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: The trust issue has been named, but the room is testing whether you will keep it visible.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, send a written repair summary naming the trust pattern, agreed behavior changes, and follow-up date

meaning.description: You are choosing visible repair follow-through.

choices.label: Let the conversation settle and check informally later in the week

meaning.description: You are choosing informal delay over visible repair.

B.escalation_text: The meeting stays positive, but the assistant later tells another team member that the real issue was avoided. The doctor believes the conversation confirmed this was only a communication problem.

second_choices.label: Keep the communication frame because the meeting stayed calm
second_choices.cost: The surface tone improves, but the assistant experiences the repair as performative.

second_choices.label: Reopen the conversation and name the trust issue directly
second_choices.cost: You must acknowledge that the first meeting stayed too safe.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: The meeting was calm, but the underlying pattern was not repaired.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, schedule a second 15-minute reset and state that the trust issue needs to be named

meaning.description: You are choosing a second, more honest repair attempt.

choices.label: Keep coaching both sides on clearer communication over the next week

meaning.description: You are choosing surface improvement over pattern repair.

C.escalation_text: Separate conversations give you more information, but each person adjusts their story. When they come together, both avoid saying what they said privately.

second_choices.label: Continue separate conversations until both people feel safer
second_choices.cost: You reduce confrontation, but you become the container for a truth they never share directly.

second_choices.label: Bring both people back together and name the gap between private and shared versions
second_choices.cost: You expose the avoidance pattern and may lose the safety of private trust.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: Private conversations revealed more truth than the shared conversation.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, hold a joint reset and ask each person to name one private concern in the room

meaning.description: You are choosing direct repair over managed safety.

choices.label: Keep working with each person separately until they are ready

meaning.description: You are choosing continued containment over direct repair.

D.escalation_text: The future workflow plan is clear, but the next busy day brings back the same pattern. The assistant hesitates again, and the doctor bypasses them again.

second_choices.label: Refine the workflow plan and add clearer role expectations
second_choices.cost: The process improves, but the relational breakdown remains untouched.

second_choices.label: Pause the workflow discussion and address the trust breakdown directly
second_choices.cost: You must admit the first meeting solved the wrong layer.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: The workflow plan did not change the behavior under pressure.

choices.label: Within 24 hours, schedule a repair follow-up focused specifically on trust, hesitation, and bypassing

meaning.description: You are choosing to address the layer that workflow did not reach.

choices.label: Give the new workflow more time before reopening the conversation

meaning.description: You are choosing more time over immediate repair.
